name: next-js-16-launchpad
description: Next.js 16 with Turbopack, Cache Components, and proxy.ts. Use for bootstrapping, migrating, and building with App Router and React 19.
capabilities: Create new Next.js 16 projects with Turbopack enabled by default
Migrate existing Next.js projects to version 16 with codemods
Implement Cache Components with use cache directive and cacheLife
Set up proxy.ts for authentication and request handling
Build Server Components and Client Components patterns
Configure streaming with Suspense for better UX
author: Calel33
securitySummary: All 849 static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate Next.js 16 documentation resource. External commands, network calls, and crypto references are all from markdown documentation showing code examples, not actual executable code with security implications.
valueStatement: Get started with Next.js 16 features including Turbopack, Cache Components, and proxy.ts. This reference includes migration guides, production patterns, and ready-to-use code examples for building modern React applications.
limitations: Does not execute code or modify files directly
Does not include runtime dependencies or npm packages
Does not provide deployment infrastructure or CI/CD pipelines
Focused on App Router patterns, not Pages Router

useCases.title: Quick start Next.js 16
useCases.description: Set up a new Next.js 16 project with recommended defaults and Turbopack enabled.

useCases.title: Migrate to proxy.ts
useCases.description: Move from middleware.ts to proxy.ts for Node.js runtime request handling.

useCases.title: Production patterns
useCases.description: Apply caching strategies, streaming, and server actions in production applications.

promptTemplates.title: New project setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create a new Next.js 16 project with Turbopack and TypeScript using the recommended defaults.

promptTemplates.title: Cache implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I use Cache Components with use cache and cacheLife in Next.js 16? Show me a complete example.

promptTemplates.title: Migration guide
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the key changes when migrating from Next.js 15 to 16? Include async params, proxy.ts migration, and config updates.

promptTemplates.title: Production patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me production patterns for Next.js 16 including streaming with Suspense, cache tags, and authenticated proxy boundaries.

bestPractices: Keep Turbopack enabled for 2-5x faster builds and faster development cycles
Extract cookies and headers before caching to avoid dynamic data in cached components
Use Suspense boundaries with Cache Components for progressive streaming
antiPatterns: Mixing 'use cache' with cookies(), headers(), or other runtime APIs
Running proxy.ts on Edge runtime instead of Node.js
Missing Suspense boundaries when Cache Components are enabled

faq.question: Is Turbopack production ready?
faq.answer: Yes, Turbopack is stable and default in Next.js 16 with 2-5x faster builds.

faq.question: How does use cache differ from PPR?
faq.answer: Cache Components replace experimental PPR with explicit opt-in caching via 'use cache'.

faq.question: Can I use middleware.ts in Next.js 16?
faq.answer: No, middleware.ts is replaced by proxy.ts for Node.js runtime request handling.

faq.question: Do I need to update async params?
faq.answer: Yes, params is now async in Next.js 16. Use await params in all page components.

faq.question: What is the minimum Node.js version?
faq.answer: Next.js 16 requires Node.js 20.9.0 or higher (LTS versions only).

faq.question: How do I cache data with specific durations?
faq.answer: Use 'use cache' with cacheLife('seconds', 'minutes', 'hours', 'days') for fine-grained control.
